current_implemented_build: V60.3.8 TrustMap Layer 1 Reference Asset Replacement
patch_type: Narrow Layer 1 graphical asset replacement over V60.3.7 full-page renderer overreach
release_chain_status: V60.3.8 is the current implemented build. It restores the original TrustMap shell, original center core, original left operational score panel, original right score/distribution area, radar rings, Layer 2 behavior, Layer 3 behavior, and TrustMap controls. It replaces only the Layer 1 asset graphics with reference-style holographic cube objects and places the selected-asset detail panel under the existing right-side score area. GitHub Pages browser QA is required after deploy.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V60.3.8. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.
v60_3_8_scope_rule: Restore the original TrustMap shell, original center core, original left operational score panel, original right score/distribution area, radar rings, Layer 2 behavior, Layer 3 behavior, and TrustMap navigation controls. Replace only the Layer 1 asset graphics with reference-style holographic cube objects. Place the selected-asset detail panel underneath the existing right-side score area.
non_negotiable_trustmap_scope: Do not replace the TrustMap page
Do not replace the center CyberShield Core
Do not remove the MJC shield/logo
Do not remove the left Operational Trust Score panel
Do not remove the upper-right score/distribution area
Do not remove Layer 2 or Layer 3
Do not replace the radar / constellation environment
Only replace the graphical representation of the Layer 1 assets

public_trustmap_language.title: CyberShield TrustMap
public_trustmap_language.subtitle: Decision Trust Universe
public_trustmap_language.rule: Do not expose builder language such as registry-driven, renders from registry, versioned render source, implementation layer, or internal version source in executive-facing TrustMap UI.

trustmap_visual_doctrine: The original TrustMap shell is preserved. The Layer 1 graphical assets receive reference-style holographic cube treatment. The selected-asset panel explains the hovered or focused Layer 1 asset and sits below the existing right-side score/distribution area.
selected_asset_panel_rule: The selected-asset panel belongs below the existing right-side score/distribution area. It changes when the user hovers or focuses a Layer 1 graphical asset. It must not replace the score/distribution area above it.
score_rule: Every visible score must be clickable and must explain or route to the model, evidence, assumptions, missing inputs, confidence, reliance risk, and decision rationale behind that score.
information_architecture_rule: Evidence tab owns the full Manual Evidence Workbench. Runtime tab owns compact evidence requirements for action. Proof Pack tab owns proof-oriented evidence trace and defensibility context. Settings owns build/version/governance/prototype metadata.
controlling_doctrine: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
model_maturity_rule: CyberShield scoring models are Expert-Derived V1 Trust Scoring Models, not Statistically Validated Trust Models. Default weights are calibratable and not authoritative until validated through test scenarios, expert review, red-team attempts, and real-world use cases.
v60_3_8_changes: replaces the V60.3.7 full-page reference renderer import in src/ui/v52-7-operational-layer.js
preserves the original TrustMap layout and scoring panels
preserves the center CyberShield Core with MJC shield/logo, organization name, and Trust Kernel language
preserves the original left Operational Trust Score panel
preserves the original upper-right score/distribution area
preserves radar rings, constellation grid, Layer 2, Layer 3, pan, zoom, and mode controls
replaces only Layer 1 asset graphics with reference-style holographic cube objects
adds a selected-asset detail panel below the right-side score/distribution area
updates the selected-asset panel on hover and focus over Layer 1 graphics
preserves Settings as the place for build/version/governance/prototype metadata

trustmap_layer1_reference_assets.interaction: selected-asset panel updates on hover and focus
trustmap_layer1_reference_assets.boundary: Static advisory visual and interaction renderer only. No live scoring, evidence retrieval, workflow automation, ticketing, notifications, backend persistence, enforcement, statistical validation, certification, compliance validation, or live integrations.

recommended_next_build.label: V60.3.9 Layer 1 Asset Fidelity Tuning
goals: browser QA the original TrustMap preservation
confirm selected panel below right score
confirm left and right score panels remain
confirm Layer 2 and Layer 3 behavior remain intact
preserve no-new-top-level-tabs and Settings-only governance metadata

implemented_release_train.v56: Trust Model Registry and Score Explanation Layer
implemented_release_train.v59.5: Internet Trust QA and Copy Guardrail Pass
implemented_release_train.v60.3.3: First-Layer Decision Brief and TrustMap Snapshot
implemented_release_train.v60.3.4: Explicit Actionability and Modal Disclosure Architecture

prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It is not connected to live enterprise telemetry, SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, cloud control planes, GRC tooling, production agent enforcement, Google Sheets, CRM sync, ticketing, notifications, marketplace systems, ad platforms, domain-intelligence systems, identity verification systems, CMMC certification systems, healthcare compliance validation systems, banking/payment systems, live monitoring, live URL ingestion, live internet retrieval, live evidence retrieval, live scoring, live claim extraction, internet claim verification, report delivery, ticketing, workflow automation, notifications, backend persistence, statistical validation, or takedown automation.
known_limitations: Layer 1 asset graphics require browser QA
Holographic assets are CSS/SVG-based and may require visual tuning
Models are expert-derived V1 and not statistically validated
No live scoring, evidence retrieval, ticketing, notifications, or workflow automation
GitHub Pages browser QA required after deploy
